metadata.service_project: Smarter Tariff - Small Asset Owner Services

threats.threat: [An external attacker] with [conveniently crafted requests] can [impersonate legitimate users], which leads to [unauthorized access to dispatch calculations], resulting in reduced [confidentiality] of [customer charging data].
threats.attack_method: An attacker crafts requests that include valid authentication tokens obtained through social engineering methods, allowing them to access sensitive endpoints of the API Gateway.
all_possible_mitigations: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) to enhance user verification.
Regularly review and rotate authentication tokens.
Generate unique API keys for developers with strict usage policies.
Employ rate limiting to control the frequency of requests from a single source.
Implement comprehensive logging and monitoring for API access and deviations from normal behavior.
Secure communication of tokens using TLS/SSL to prevent interception.

threats.threat: [A malicious insider] with [developer access] can [alter stored dispatch results], which leads to [incorrect data being served], resulting in reduced [integrity] of [customer charging data].
threats.attack_method: A developer with access to the Valkey Cache may use privileged permissions to modify cache data without logging these modifications.
all_possible_mitigations: Implement fine-grained access controls to restrict modifications only to authorized users.
Use versioning of cached data to prevent unauthorized changes from going undetected.
Apply data validation rules for caching processes to ensure data integrity.
Audit logs for all changes made to the Valkey Cache to track unauthorized modifications.
Implement a cache eviction strategy to refresh data periodically and minimize the risk of stale data.

threats.threat: [An external attacker] with [exploited endpoint vulnerabilities] can [retrieve sensitive data], which leads to [customer charging history being exposed], resulting in reduced [confidentiality] of [customer charging data].
threats.attack_method: An attacker explores the API Gateway for weak endpoints that do not have proper authorization checks in place, allowing them to access customer data.
all_possible_mitigations: Conduct regular penetration testing to identify vulnerabilities in the API endpoints.
Implement strict input validation and sanitize output to prevent injection attacks.
Ensure all endpoints implement authentication and authorization processes appropriately.
Utilize API gateway security policies, such as IP whitelisting and blacklisting.
Use encryption to protect sensitive data both at rest and in transit.

threats.threat: [An external attacker] with [high-volume automated scripts] can [flood the API Gateway with requests], which leads to [service unavailability], resulting in reduced [availability] of [customer services].
threats.attack_method: A botnet is set up to automatically send thousands of requests to the API Gateway, overwhelming it and preventing legitimate users from accessing the service.
all_possible_mitigations: Deploy web application firewalls (WAF) to filter out malicious traffic effectively.
Implement rate limiting to reduce the impact of flooding attacks on the API gateway.
Use resource quotas to limit the amount of processing power available per user session.
Setup a content delivery network (CDN) to absorb excessive traffic volume.
Develop an incident response plan specifically for DDoS attacks.

threats.threat: [A compromised developer] with [stolen credentials] can [gain unauthorized access to sensitive resources], which leads to [misuse of the AWS environment], resulting in reduced [confidentiality] of [sensitive assets].
threats.attack_method: An attacker steals developer credentials through phishing attacks and uses them to access the AWS Console, where they can modify resource configurations.
all_possible_mitigations: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all developer accounts.
Regularly rotate access keys and passwords to minimize risks of long-term exposure.
Conduct security awareness training for developers to identify phishing attempts.
Use IAM roles with least privilege principle for developers.
Monitor AWS CloudTrail logs for unauthorized access attempts and patterns.

threats.threat: [A malicious insider] with [access to CloudWatch] can [delete logs], which leads to [loss of audit trail], resulting in reduced [accountability] of [system actions].
threats.attack_method: An authorized user accesses CloudWatch and deletes specific logs related to their activities to hide malicious actions.
all_possible_mitigations: Implement immutable logging to prevent modification or deletion of log data.
Restrict access to log deletion features to essential personnel only.
Set up alerts for unusual log activity, such as deletions or modifications.
Conduct regular inspections of log integrity with automated tools.
Maintain logs in a separate secure environment to reduce insider threat exposure.
